content.full_text:
May 30, 1990


Rita Walters
Board of Education
City of Los Angeles
P.O. Box 3307
Los Angeles, CA  90051

                                        Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                             Our File No. A-90-308

Dear Ms. Walters:

     This is in response to your letter requesting advice regard-
ing your responsibilities as a member of the Los Angeles Unified
School District Board of Education under the new "personal use"
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

                              QUESTION

     May campaign funds be used to pay for your travel, accommoda-
tions and conference fees for a conference concerning middle
schools as a method to relieve school overcrowding, sponsored by
the California League of Middle Schools and the Hawaii Association
of Middle Schools?

                              CONCLUSION

     Expenditures made for a conference for public officials
intended to improve their ability to perform their official duties
and to provide information to be used in the performance of the
official's governmental duties are directly related to a
governmental purpose. Consequently, campaign funds may be used to
pay to attend the conference. Further, use of campaign funds to
pay for ordinary and necessary travel expenses to and from the
conference is also permitted.


_______________________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory refer-
ences are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-90-308
Page 2

FACTS

You have asked whether you may use campaign funds to pay for
travel, accommodations and program fees for a conference on middle
schools offered by the California League of Middle Schools and the
Hawaii Association of Middle Schools.  The middle school is an
alternative to a two year junior high school system in which the
junior high school is replaced with a three year middle school.
The restructuring of the school system results in less crowding at
the high school level.  The conference is scheduled to run from
June 27 through June 29 and will concern such topics as the
scheduling and the characteristics and instructional practices of
middle schools.

ANALYSIS

Effective January 1, 1990, the Act was amended by Senate Bill
1431 to include new provisions that regulate the appropriate use
of campaign funds.  (Section 85800, et seq., the "personal use"
law.)  The purpose of the legislation was "to ensure that
candidates, elected officers and the people clearly recognize ac-
ceptable and unacceptable uses of campaign funds."  (Senate Elec-
tions Committee Analysis of SB 1431.)

The general rule of the new personal use law is that any
expenditure of campaign funds must be, at a minimum, reasonably
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
(Section 85801.)  However, where an expenditure confers a
substantial personal benefit on the candidate or officeholder, the
expenditure must be directly related to a political, legislative,
or governmental purpose.2/  (Section 85801.)

Program Fees

Where campaign funds are used to pay a program fee for your
attendance at an educational conference, you have received a
personal benefit equivalent to the payment.  According to the
facts you provided, the tuition for the conference is $100.
Consequently, for the expenditure of campaign funds to be permis-
sible there must be a direct relationship between the expenditure
a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.  (Section
85801.)

Since the information you obtain at the conference offered by
the California League of Middle Schools and Hawaii Association of
Middle Schools directly relates to your role as a board member of
the Los Angeles Unified School District and will be used in the
performance of your governmental duties, it appears there is a
direct relationship between the expenditure of campaign funds for

2/  A substantial personal benefit is defined as a direct personal
benefit to the candidate or officeholder of $100 or more.  (Sec-
tion 85800(b)(2).)

Our File No. A-90-308
Page 3

the conference and a governmental purpose associate with your
elected office. Consequently, campaign funds may be used to pay
program fees to attend the conference.

Travel and Accommodations

Section 85802(a)(1) provides:

     (a) Campaign funds shall not be used to pay
or reimburse the candidate, the elected officer, or
employees or staff of the campaign committee of the
elected officer's governmental agency for travel
expenses and necessary accommodations except when
these expenditures are directly related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose.

     (1) For purposes of this section, payments or
reimbursements for travel and necessary accommoda-
tions shall be considered as directly related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose if
the payments would meet standards similar to the
standards of the Internal Revenue Service pursuant
to Sections 162 and 274 of the Internal Revenue
Code for deductions of travel expenses under the
federal income tax law.

(Emphasis added.)

     According to Section 85802(a)(1), the use of campaign funds
for payments or reimbursements for travel and necessary accommoda-
tions is directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose if the payments meet standards similar to the
standards for the deduction of travel expenses pursuant to
Internal Revenue Code Sections 162 and 274.

     Internal Revenue Code Section 162(a)(2) sets forth a general
rule which permits the deduction of all ordinary and necessary
traveling expenses while away from home in the pursuit of a trade
or business. According to your facts the travel expenses are
ordinary and necessary expenses for a governmental purpose which
are associated with your official duties. Consequently, use of
campaign funds for the travel expenses is permitted.37/

     Please note, however that accommodations outside the duration
of the conference are personal expenses that should be paid with

_______________________

3/  It should be recognized that the Commission has recently as-
sumed jurisdiction over the laws regarding the permissible uses of
campaign funds. In the near future the Commission will begin
considering and approving regulations interpreting these laws.
Therefore, it is possible that the Commission may adopt regula-
tions in the future which may be at variance with the advice given
in this letter.

Our File No. A-90-308
Page 4

personal funds. Thus, after the conference ends on the 29th of
June, accommodations for any subsequent days may not be paid with
campaign funds.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
